page_summary: Page 1: Details of accessories for the product (parts diagram only). Page 2: This page outlines the chair installation steps before using, covering the base, casters, gas lift, mechanism, footrest rails, and the seat. Page 3: This page covers attaching connectors, armrests, headrest, footrest, and lumbar pillow to the chair back/backrest assembly.

instruction_steps.text: Insert the casters. Install the gas lift.
Install the mechanism and the footrest rails.
Place the seat on the gas lift.
Install the connector on the chair back.
Install the connector into the rear end of mechanism.
Attach the headrest to the backrest.
Put the lumbar pillow on the backrest. Complete.

inventory.inferred_function: Foundational support platform for the chair; serves as the mounting or anchoring surface for casters and the gas lift, enabling the seat assembly to be raised and stabilized.
inventory.function_rationale: Step 1 explicitly installs the base. Step 2 mentions inserting casters and installing the gas lift, which would typically attach to or sit on the base to provide mobility and height adjustment for the seat. Step 4 places the seat on the gas lift, implying the base supports the gas lift system as a whole. Therefore, the base functions as the foundational frame that anchors other height-adjustment and mobility components.
inventory.one_sentence_summary: A five-armed, star-like base with a central hub that anchors wheels/legs and supports the gas lift for height adjustment.
inventory.shape: five-armed star-like base with a central hub
inventory.size_relative: medium (fits under chair; each arm extends outward from center)
inventory.thickness_or_depth: thin to moderate (flat structural plate with lattice/ribbing visible on arms)
inventory.edge_profile: smooth edges with rounded tips at arm ends; some angled intersections where arms meet hub
inventory.surface_texture: central hub and arms show lattice reinforcements; metallic or sturdy plate texture
protrusions: central hub hole area for mounting
grooves_slots_holes: multiple small circular holes along each arm (for screws/bolts)
central hole area at hub for mounting
slot patterns consistent with joint connections
inventory.symmetry: radially symmetric around central hub (5-fold symmetry) with identical arms
orientation_cues: arms extend outward—fivefold symmetry helps orientation by feel of five equal arms

differences: pedestal is typically a single vertical column or a flat support, not a five-arm star with a central hub and multiple arm holes; base has five extending arms and distributed mounting holes
frame is usually a continuous perimeter or rectangular/oval structure; base is star-shaped with radial arms and open spaces for wheel attachment
chassis is a larger enclosing framework; base is smaller, with five arms and host to mounting holes for casters and gas lift
foundation plate is flat and often rectangular; base is star-shaped with lattice and multiple arms, more complex topology
base plate is generally flat and single surface; base here has five arms and central hub, plus holes along arms

action_relevant_notes: To identify by touch: feel a central hub with five outward arms; locate multiple small circular holes along each arm and a central hole at the hub for mounting hardware.
When installing, align arms outward from center to match corresponding casters and rail/gas lift interfaces; the central hub is the primary anchor point for mounting hardware.
If two similar parts exist, use the five-arm star shape and radial symmetry as primary identifiers.
uncertainties: Exact hole diameters and thread specifications are not readable from the crop
Some features are partially occluded by page layout; interpretation based on visible outlines and typical chair base designs

inventory.inferred_function: Provide mobility to the chair by allowing the base to roll on the floor when attached to the base.
inventory.function_rationale: The steps show casters are installed as part of the base assembly and are placed before the seat is placed on the gas lift, which indicates their role is to enable movement of the chair by rolling. They are not described as structural supports or the lifting mechanism, which supports the inference that their primary function is to allow rolling mobility.
inventory.one_sentence_summary: Five caster assemblies extend from a five-spoked base, each ending in a small wheel housed in a cylindrical housing with a short stem.
inventory.shape: five-armed star base with five extending spokes; each spoke ends with a small caster assembly
inventory.size_relative: casters appear small relative to chair base; spokes appear evenly spaced around central hub
inventory.thickness_or_depth: spokes are thin; caster stems are short
inventory.edge_profile: spokes have rounded outer edges; caster housings are cylindrical
inventory.surface_texture: likely smooth on metal surfaces; wheels are finished to roll
protrusions: caster stems protrude from the end of each spoke; small wheel axles visible within caster housings
grooves_slots_holes: no visible holes or slots in the visible area of the caster housings; no grooves observed on the components in this crop
inventory.symmetry: radially symmetric around central hub (fivefold symmetry)
distinctive_landmarks: cylindrical caster housings at each spoke end

differences: casters have a vertical stem and housing enabling swiveling; wheels mounted within a rotating caster housing
glides are stationary feet with flat contact surfaces; casters have rotating wheels and a stem
this part resembles typical caster assemblies with a wheel on a stem rather than fixed feet; central hub with five spokes indicates mobility via wheels

action_relevant_notes: To identify by touch: locate the central hub and five spoke arms; feel for cylindrical housings at each spoke end with a small wheel inside.
When using: roll the chair to move; the wheels should rotate within their housings as you push or steer the chair.
uncertainties: Exact number of casters confirmed as five based on visible spokes; could be more or less in non-visible portions of the base
Dimensions and scale of wheels and stems are not provided in this crop

inventory.inferred_function: Provides vertical height-adjustable support for the seat by a gas spring/piston, enabling the seat to be raised or lowered and supporting the seat when placed on it.
inventory.function_rationale: The steps show the gas lift being installed with casters and then having the seat placed on it, indicating it serves as the adjustable support column for seat height. There is no other component described that would provide this vertical adjustment, so the gas lift is inferred to be the height-adjustment mechanism.
inventory.one_sentence_summary: The gas lift is the vertical height-adjustment column beneath the seat; in this crop it is not visible, only a panel is shown.
inventory.shape: If present, the gas lift would be a cylindrical column under the seat; in this crop the gas lift is not observable (only a rectangular panel is visible).
inventory.size_relative: Not observable in this crop since the target part is not shown.
inventory.edge_profile: Not observable in this crop; panel edges are rectangular.
inventory.surface_texture: Not observable in this crop; metallic/cylindrical texture cannot be confirmed.
protrusions: None observed in this crop for the gas lift.
grooves_slots_holes: None observed in this crop for the gas lift.
inventory.symmetry: Not determinable from this crop; gas lift symmetry would depend on full assembly.
distinctive_landmarks: QR code box is visible on the panel, but it is not part of the gas lift and provides a separate landmark for this crop.

differences: gas spring is typically a sealed cylinder with end caps
gas lift is located under the seat and functions as height-adjustment column; in this crop the gas lift itself is not visible, only a panel is visible
gas cylinder typically a larger, isolated cylindrical body; here the gas lift would be integrated as a vertical adjustment component under the seat
not observable in this crop
pneumatic lift implies tilting/raising mechanism; shape and placement differ from a standard gas lift under the seat; not visible here
pneumatic cylinder similar in function but different form factor; not observable in this crop
lift cylinder is a generic term; form factors vary; not visible in this crop

action_relevant_notes: In steps 2 and 4, locate the vertical assembly under the seat (the gas lift) when the base with casters is in place. If you can feel a vertical cylindrical component, that is the gas lift to adjust height.
If the component is not yet attached, you will likely feel a panel or housing before the cylindrical lift is encountered in the full assembly.
uncertainties: Gas lift component is not visible in the provided crop; geometry cannot be determined from this image alone.

inventory.inferred_function: Seat adjustment and linkage component that anchors and enables interaction between the seat, gas lift, and footrest rails (likely a tilt/recline or pivot mechanism).
inventory.function_rationale: The mechanism is installed together with the footrest rails in step 3, prior to seating placement on the gas lift in step 4, indicating it serves as the structural linkage that enables seat adjustment and supports attachment to the seat assembly. The gas lift provides vertical positioning, while the mechanism likely translates control inputs into seat movement.
inventory.one_sentence_summary: A structural linkage component for seat adjustment, currently not visually identifiable in the provided crop, but functionally anchors seat to rails and gas lift.

No confirmable visual cues in this crop; function inference suggests linkage role rather than a dedicated recline housing.
Current crop offers no tactile/shape details to differentiate from other linkages.
No identifiable swivel features in the visible area.

action_relevant_notes: If you can locate mounting points or interfaces to the seat rails or gas lift by touch, check for flat surfaces with possible bolt holes or tabs that would align with a mid-line pivot.

Because geometry is not visible here, treat this as a rigid linkage likely offering one or more attachment interfaces; confirm by locating fixed connections to seat rails or base in your tactile exploration.
uncertainties: The crop shows only QR area and generic page header; no distinct mechanism geometry is visible to describe shape, holes, slots, or protrusions.

inventory.inferred_function: Support and provide a resting place for the user's feet as part of the chair's footrest assembly, attached to the mechanism to extend seating comfort and stability.
inventory.function_rationale: The rails are installed in step 3 alongside the 'mechanism' to form the footrest portion of the seat assembly, indicating their role is to create a supported surface for feet within the chair's adjustment/comfort system.
inventory.one_sentence_summary: Elongated rails connected to the footrest mechanism provide the feet-resting surface and are felt as parallel touch points near the seating mechanism.
inventory.shape: Not clearly visible in the provided crop; shape not observable here.
grooves_slots_holes: No holes/slots/grooves observable for the footrest rails in the crop
orientation_cues: Locate near the seating mechanism; feel for two parallel rails extending from the mechanism
May run along the sides of the seat area when extended
distinctive_landmarks: Attached to the footrest mechanism; contrasts with other components like base/seat

differences: Footrest rails are elongated support elements that connect to the mechanism; a separate footrest would be a platform or surface you rest feet on, whereas rails are the supporting rails that form that surface.
Footrest bar is typically a single bar; rails imply a pair or longer extended members that provide a surface along their length.
Footboard is usually a flat panel; rails are slender elongated members forming the edge/surface rather than a full panel.
Toe rest is a smaller, forward-anchored element; rails are part of the main resting surface and run alongside the mechanism rather than projecting as a separate small piece.
Terminology overlap; emphasis here is on the rails forming the surface via attachment to the mechanism, whereas other terms may describe a broader or differently shaped support component.

action_relevant_notes: To identify by touch: seek two parallel, elongated bars connected to the seat/footrest mechanism; they should feel like a pair of rails forming a resting surface when the mechanism is in its resting/extended position.
If the mechanism is adjusted, the rails may move with it, confirming their role in the footrest surface usage.
uncertainties: Footrest rails geometry not shown in the current crop; evaluation relies on other pages or extended views

inventory.inferred_function: Provides the seating surface that supports the user and rests on the gas lift assembly, completing the chair seating area.
inventory.function_rationale: Step 4 places the seat on the gas lift, indicating its role as the uppermost surface for sitting and transferring weight to the gas lift mechanism.
inventory.one_sentence_summary: A flat, rounded-edged seating cushion that forms the top surface you sit on, positioned above the gas lift.
inventory.shape: flat cushion with rounded edges, elongated rectangular/oval form
inventory.size_relative: medium; width comparable to the base workspace shown; appears similar to standard chair seat size

inventory.symmetry: likely symmetric left-right; no obvious offset features visible
distinctive_landmarks: flat seating surface with rounded edges; exposed cushion portion on top side (no visible lattice or skeletal framework on top)

top geometry: backrest is typically vertical and textured for back contact; seat is horizontal and flat on top
structure: base is a supporting platform under the seat; seat is the top cushion surface

position: located at sides as supports for arms; seat is the main central surface to sit on, without side protrusions on the top surface

action_relevant_notes: Place weight downward onto the top surface to transfer load to the gas lift.
Since underside and attachment features are not visible, confirm seating surface rests securely on gas lift by gentle weight application during use.

inventory.inferred_function: A mounting/connecting interface that attaches the chair back to the mechanism, serving as a coupling between the back assembly and the mechanism to enable proper attachment of ancillary components.
inventory.function_rationale: Steps show the connector being installed on the chair back (step 5) and then installed into the rear end of the mechanism (step 6), indicating it is the linking piece that joins the back assembly to the mechanism rather than a standalone component. This implies a role as a connector or bracket that aligns and attaches the back to the mechanism.

inventory.inferred_function: Acts as the mounting and support surface for accessories and mechanisms, serving as the backrest component of the chair where connectors, headrest, lumbar pillow, and other attachments are mounted.
inventory.function_rationale: The part receives a connector (step 5), supports attachments like a headrest (step 8) and lumbar pillow (step 10), and serves as the surface to which mechanism connectors attach (step 6 references the mechanism via the connector). This indicates it is the primary back-support structure and attachment point for related components.

This suggests it is the core structural or functional housing for the chair's adjustable features.

inventory.inferred_function: Provide integrated support for the user's arms by attaching to the chair frame, offering a resting surface and contributing to overall chair ergonomics.
inventory.function_rationale: Step 7 calls to 'Install the armrests', indicating their role as a user-support surface rather than a structural brace. Surrounding steps reference other comfort components, situating armrests as ergonomic features for arm resting and posture stabilization.
inventory.one_sentence_summary: A pair of symmetrical, padded armrests that attach to the chair sides to provide a resting surface for forearms.
inventory.shape: Two curved padded arms extending horizontally from the chair sides, forming symmetrical supports for forearms.
inventory.size_relative: Medium relative to the seat and backrest; length extends from the seat to roughly the midpoint of the user’s forearms.
inventory.thickness_or_depth: Thick cushioned top surface with a likely firm underlying mounting structure.
inventory.edge_profile: Smooth, rounded outer edge with a distinct upper padding surface; underside shows a thinner mounting beam.
inventory.surface_texture: Top surface: soft padded upholstery suitable for forearm contact; underside: harder mounting area.
protrusions: bolt heads protruding from the mounting bracket; washers and a nut/cover near attachment points
grooves_slots_holes: Multiple mounting holes on the bracket where the armrest attaches to the chair (visible as punched holes); circular holes for bolts in the mounting plate; slot-like gaps may be present where bolts pass through
inventory.symmetry: Left and right armrests are mirror images; symmetrical design and attachment.
orientation_cues: Arrows indicating alignment on the mounting area; M8*40 screws specified for attachment
distinctive_landmarks: Bolts and bolt heads protruding from the mounting bracket; washers and nuts near attachment points; upper padded surface distinct from other components

differences: Headrest is located at the top/back region, not at chair sides; armrest has a horizontal side extension with a padded top surface and visible mounting bolts on the bracket.
Footrest typically extends downward from chair front area for leg support; armrest extends horizontally from sides with padding for forearms and a different mounting pattern.
Backrest provides vertical support behind the user; armrest provides lateral side support with a top padding surface and bolt-based mounting at the chair sides.
Lumbar pillow is a separate cushion placed along the lower back; armrest is a fixed side surface with padding for forearms, attached via side brackets.

action_relevant_notes: Align armrests with chair sides during installation; ensure access to bolt heads and washers for secure fastening; check padding orientation for comfortable forearm rest.
uncertainties: Exact numeric dimensions are not provided; inference based on bolt labels (M8*40) and relative component size to chair.

inventory.inferred_function: Provides head support when seated by attaching to the backrest, offering stability and comfort for the user's head.
inventory.function_rationale: Step 8 states the headrest is attached to the backrest, implying its role is to support the head as part of the chair’s backrest assembly. The placement suggests a supportive, cushioned component designed to contact the occupant's head.
inventory.one_sentence_summary: A thin, smoothly curved edge along the backrest area that serves as a head-support component when seated.

differences: neck pillow is typically a standalone cushion intended to support the neck away from the backrest; this headrest is described as attaching to and part of the backrest, suggesting integrated support rather than a separate portable cushion.

inventory.inferred_function: Serves as the main rear support surface of the chair that interfaces with the mechanism via a connector and provides mounting surfaces for accessories (headrest and lumbar pillow) while also supporting attachment of the back-related components (armrests are not directly attached to it in these steps).
inventory.function_rationale: The steps show the backrest acting as the surface where the connector is installed into the chair back (step 5) and then into the mechanism (step 6), and as the attachment point for the headrest (step 8) and lumbar pillow (step 10). This indicates the backrest functions as a structural back support that also accommodates accessory components.

page_summaries.summary: Details of accessories for the product (parts diagram only).
This page outlines the chair installation steps before using, covering the base, casters, gas lift, mechanism, footrest rails, and the seat.
This page covers attaching connectors, armrests, headrest, footrest, and lumbar pillow to the chair back/backrest assembly.
